reason: The named authority ('an amendment that adds a ruling updates the Decision enumeration' + template §2 testable sub-decisions) does not uniquely determine a fix: §2's Decision statement already commits schema provisioning 'orchestrated by a driver-less loader that leverages knowledge-service's ports' and 'sole holder of the graph driver for data-definition,' and §2.2 is itself an enumerated Decision sub-decision surfacing the provisioning capability. Whether the top-line sentence must additionally name the capability-addition and deploy-time-script retirement, and in what words, is an authoring choice the authority does not entail.
authority_locus: author-decision-record SKILL, 'Body integrity' — 'An amendment that adds a ruling updates the Decision enumeration'; ADR template §2 (Decision as testable sub-decisions)

reason: The finding names a mild internal-tension between §2.5's flat phrasing and §5.3's hedge but neither ADR-002 §5.2 nor §5.3 dictates a unique replacement wording; conforming would require choosing among several hedge phrasings — an unratified authorial choice.
authority_locus: ADR-002 §5.2 (managed-Aura backup/restore = development runtime only; production deferred) + ADR-004 §5.3 (own hedge)

reason: no document change recorded in the prior pass

existing_claim: §2.7 states 'read-exclusion of provisional ground truth is a schema-level invariant — a provisional authoritative-source node is excluded from ground-truth synthesis until officially entered, homed in the schema's read-discipline invariant set.' This asserts a schema-level invariant as an existing home, but the record cites DDR-002 §7 only generically (§7 Cross-References) and does not name an existing DDR-002 read-discipline invariant covering provisional-vs-official authoritative-source nodes; §6 check 6 concedes the mechanism is 'review-judgment until the fidelity-gate and write-authorship mechanics land, at which point check 6 gains a mechanized read-discipline mirror.' The decision claims a schema-invariant home that the substrate does not yet contain — the consequence (a new DDR-002 invariant) is a decision this ADR commits without surfacing that DDR-002 must be amended to carry it.
incoming_claim: §2.7 states 'read-exclusion of provisional ground truth is a schema-level invariant — a provisional authoritative-source node is excluded from ground-truth synthesis until officially entered, homed in the schema's read-discipline invariant set and mirrored by the port's behavioral contract.' The DDR-002 §7 read-discipline invariant set contains only #9 (proposal-visibility), #19 (conditional-consumption), and the retracted-node read-discipline — none of which govern a provisional-versus-official authoritative-source node, and DDR-002 §5's applicability_state enumeration is {unconditional, conditional} with no provisional value or marker. The consequence of this decision — a new DDR-002 read-discipline invariant and a new provisional state — is committed by cross-reference as an existing home, but the substrate does not carry it; the decision commits DDR-002 to a new invariant it has not itself ruled, and the ADR does not surface that DDR-002 must be amended to carry it.

existing_claim: §2.7 declares 'this ADR realizes that control for instantiation and is the record the governance record names as the home of the ingestion mechanics,' and §7 states this record 'is the "forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record" that governance record names.' ADR-008 (substrate) names its home as 'The forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record — owns the ingestion mechanics realizing the authoritative-source ingestion control (§2.2).' But ADR-008 §2.2's realization route for authoritative-source ingestion reads 'The forthcoming ingestion decision record; the knowledge-service ingestion port' — the mechanics (provisional-state representation, official-entry transition) are explicitly deferred by ADR-004 §2.7 to 'the knowledge-service design's.' ADR-004 both claims to be the home of the ingestion mechanics and defers those mechanics downstream, so the decision does not decide what its claim to be that home asserts it decides.
incoming_claim: §2.7 declares 'this ADR realizes that control for instantiation and is the record the governance record names as the home of the ingestion mechanics,' and §7 states this record 'is the "forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record" that governance record names.' Yet §2.7's final sentence explicitly defers the mechanics — 'The port-level mechanics — how provisional state is represented and how the official-entry transition is enforced — are the knowledge-service design's.' The record both claims to be the home of the ingestion mechanics and routes those mechanics downstream in the same section, so the decision does not decide what its own claim-to-be-that-home asserts it decides; the authority ADR-008 routed here is only partially discharged.

existing_claim: §2.2 states 'Ownership of constraint versioning over the platform's life ... is a forward question ... with its policy not settled here.' This is an undecided item carried inside the Decision section rather than surfaced as an explicit named gap or deferred sub-decision with a firing trigger; §5.3 restates it as a risk but neither locus routes it to a tracking home. A forward question left in the Decision body without a declared resolution path is a scope-hygiene defect at this altitude — the record does not declare where or when this sub-decision is decided.
incoming_claim: §2.2 states 'Ownership of constraint versioning over the platform's life — how the provisioning capability evolves a live instance's constraints — is a forward question, governed by construction ... but with its policy not settled here.' This is an undecided sub-decision left inside the Decision section; §5.3 restates it as a risk asserting it is 'a named forward question, not a silent gap,' but neither locus declares a tracking home or a firing trigger for when this sub-decision is decided. A forward question carried in the Decision body without a declared resolution path is a scope-hygiene gap at this altitude.

existing_claim: Attacked whether §2.8 smuggles a write-authorship decision beyond its altitude or leaves the loader's role ambiguous against ADR-002's author/executor principle. The section holds: it declares the author as the source artifact's accountable authority (curator / fidelity verifier), names the gateway as sole executor and never author, and explicitly places the loader as 'neither author nor executor ... the relay' — cleanly instantiating ADR-002 §2.6's principle for the instantiation case without re-deciding it. Claim-fidelity is intact; the decision decides exactly the authorship assignment it claims.
incoming_claim: Attacked whether §2.8 smuggles a write-authorship decision beyond this record's claim or leaves the loader's role ambiguous against ADR-002 §2.6's author/executor principle. It holds: the author is declared as the source artifact's accountable authority (curator / fidelity verifier), the gateway is named sole executor and never author, and the loader is explicitly placed as 'neither author nor executor ... the relay.' The decision decides exactly the authorship assignment it claims — a clean instantiation-case realization of ADR-002 §2.6, without relocating authorship onto gateway or loader.

existing_claim: Attacked for the exact scope-creep the substrate's own conventions warn against — whether 'fresh, not migrated' silently licenses a destructive reset, and whether the record conflates origin-binding with loader behavior. It holds: §2.3 binds 'fresh/no migration' explicitly to the graph's origin and states it 'is not a license to reset or rebuild a populated instance (§2.4),' and §2.4 separately commits additive-by-supersession selected per-node by current state with 'no destructive-reset operation.' The two decisions are cleanly separated, each surfaced, neither over-claiming; the Consequences and Compliance sections carry both faithfully.
incoming_claim: Attacked for the exact scope-creep the substrate's own conventions warn against — whether 'fresh, not migrated' silently licenses a destructive reset, conflating origin-binding with loader behavior. It holds: §2.3 binds 'fresh/no migration' explicitly to the graph's origin and states it 'is not a license to reset or rebuild a populated instance (§2.4),' and §2.4 separately commits additive-by-supersession with 'no destructive-reset operation.' The two decisions are cleanly separated, each surfaced in §2, §5.2, and §6 checks 3 and 8, neither over-claiming.

existing_claim: §2.4 states instantiation 'is one operation whose behavior is selected per node by the graph's current state, not by a loader mode: against an empty instance every business key is new and is created; against a populated instance a changed business key supersedes its predecessor under the schema's supersession discipline.' DDR-002 §6 scopes 'Option A supersession' to 'versioned-ground-truth types (Catalog, Standards, RateCard, CostFactor, PlaneDefinition)' — not all KG node classes. Environment-plane nodes age via freshness (`observed_at`) and Operational `ObservedPattern`s distill update-in-place (DDR-002 §2.2, §2.3), which are not supersession under §6. The §2.4 claim that a changed business key 'supersedes its predecessor under the schema's supersession discipline' as the uniform instantiation behavior does not hold for the non-versioned planes the schema defines, and the ADR does not carve them out; the design as written would not apply supersession uniformly if implemented exactly against the schema's actual per-plane temporal model.
incoming_claim: §2.4 states instantiation 'is one operation whose behavior is selected per node by the graph's current state, not by a loader mode: against an empty instance every business key is new and is created; against a populated instance a changed business key supersedes its predecessor under the schema's supersession discipline.' DDR-002 §6 scopes 'Option A supersession' explicitly to 'versioned-ground-truth types (Catalog, Standards, RateCard, CostFactor, PlaneDefinition)' — not all KG node classes. DDR-002 §2.2 Environment nodes age via freshness (observed_at) and §2.3 Operational ObservedPatterns distill update-in-place, neither of which is supersession under §6. The §2.4 claim that a changed business key uniformly 'supersedes its predecessor under the schema's supersession discipline' would not, if implemented exactly against the schema's per-plane temporal model, hold for the non-versioned planes; the ADR carves no exception.

existing_claim: The decision commits knowledge-service to owning a schema-provisioning capability that lays down DDL constraints, but this capability is not committed by any decision record knowledge-service implements. SDD-001 §1 states the service's readiness checks presuppose the schema is already present ('schema metadata loaded — the PlaneDefinition registry and constraint/validation metadata the write paths enforce against') and SDD-001 nowhere assigns knowledge-service authorship of constraint provisioning; §2.2 states 'provisioning runs before readiness reports green' — a service-shape commitment that deserves its own decision at the service-design altitude before an ADR asserts it as settled platform posture.
incoming_claim: §2.2 commits knowledge-service to owning a schema-provisioning capability: 'knowledge-service ... owns a schema-provisioning capability exercised over the graph driver it already holds ... The driver-less loader utility invokes this capability.' This is a service-shape commitment that constrains the platform posture, yet SDD-001 §2.1 (Owned) enumerates no provisioning capability, and §7's own cross-reference concedes SDD-001 'is to gain the schema-provisioning operation' — a commitment that deserves its own decision at the service-design altitude before an ADR asserts it as settled platform posture. This ADR commits a position (knowledge-service gains a DDL-provisioning operation, and readiness-gating is re-characterized to run provisioning before green) that the record it depends on has not yet decided.

reason: The finding requires surfacing two commitments (the schema-provisioning capability and the deploy-time-script supersession) into the §2 Decision enumeration, but the authority does not determine the exact wording, placement, or degree of elaboration of the added Decision text — that is an authoring choice among multiple conforming phrasings, not a uniquely-determined edit.
authority_locus: author-decision-record SKILL, 'Body integrity' — 'An amendment that adds a ruling updates the Decision enumeration'; the Decision-block-as-contract-index rule

authority_conformed: ADR-004 §2.5/§5.3 — 'the RG is not source-recoverable and must not be presumed so — its durability across instance loss rests on instance-level backup and restore ... deferred (see §5.3)'

reason: The named authority establishes the incumbent practice is absent, but does not uniquely determine the conforming edit: the offending clause 'the earlier practice of applying constraints from a deploy-time script is superseded — the design wins over the incidental build-time convenience' could be deleted, softened to a hypothetical, or re-grounded on the DDR-002 conformance-harness carrier — multiple conforming rewrites, an unratified choice.
authority_locus: ADR-004 §1 (schema data-definition half 'has not yet been applied') + DDR-002 §7 Current-state (conformance harness as constraint carrier; no deploy-time constraint-application script named)

reason: ADR-008 §2.2 fixes distillation's own review control but does not determine how ADR-004 §2.7 (scoped to the authoritative-source fidelity gate) must phrase its non-application; conforming would require choosing whether/how to enumerate the distillation class — an unratified authorial decision, not a determined edit.
authority_locus: ADR-008 §2.2 (four-class calibration; Operational distillation carries a per-distinct-lesson substantive human review, not ungated)

reason: The cited discipline ('an amendment that adds a ruling updates the Decision enumeration') governs amendments; this is original authoring (v0.1.0, no prior enumeration to update). Whether the provisioning capability and script supersession are distinct sub-decisions warranting new §2 enumeration lines, and what their wording would be, is an authorial design choice the authority does not uniquely determine — the §2 sentence already names 'schema provisioning and data load alike' and provisioning is elaborated in §2.2.
authority_locus: author-decision-record-SKILL, 'An amendment that adds a ruling updates the Decision enumeration' (body-integrity discipline); ADR template §2 (Decision as testable sub-decisions)

reason: The named authority requires a deferred obligation to get 'a home with a firing trigger,' but neither the SKILL nor the ADR template determines which specific home (which owning design/decision record) or which trigger fires this sub-decision — that is an unratified choice the authority does not uniquely supply.
authority_locus: author-decision-record SKILL — 'Every deferred obligation gets a home with a firing trigger' (amendment-lifecycle discipline); ADR template §6/§7 conditions-and-tracking apparatus
